data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-5ca2b0af">
    <title>beads: sort ready queues by queue-rank within priority</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
FEAT-004 and SD-004 now require `queue-rank` to order ready beads within a priority bucket, but the store still ignores queue-rank. Operators currently have to change `priority` to force a bead to the top, which changes urgency semantics and can reorder unrelated work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:104-119 defines canonical ready-queue ordering as priority, queue-rank, created_at, id, and says queue-rank must not cross priority or bypass execution filters.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:196-209 defines the same deterministic queue derivation and numeric compatibility rules.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1346-1355 exposes Ready and ReadyExecution, but both delegate to readyFiltered without queue-rank-specific behavior.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1702-1711 sorts only by Priority, CreatedAt, and ID.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small queue-rank parser/helper in cli/internal/bead (acceptable names: QueueRank, queueRank, or parseQueueRank) that accepts integer values and numeric strings from Bead.Extra["queue-rank"].
- Update sortBeadsForQueue to sort by priority asc, explicit queue-rank asc with missing rank after explicit rank inside the same priority, created_at asc, then id asc.
- Keep ReadyExecution filtering unchanged: blocked, cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible=false, and epic-only beads must be excluded before sorting.
- Add focused store tests in cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go or queue-specific test file.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add queue-rank as a Bead struct field or known schema field.
- Do not implement `ddx bead queue` CLI commands in this bead.
- Do not change priority validation, dependency semantics, or claim semantics.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestReadySortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies an explicit lower `queue-rank` sorts before unranked and higher-ranked beads of the same priority.
2. TestReadyExecutionSortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies ReadyExecution uses the same queue-rank order after execution-ready filtering.
3. TestQueueRankDoesNotCrossPriorityBoundary verifies a ranked P1 bead never sorts before an unranked P0 bead.
4. TestQueueRankMissingSortsAfterExplicitRankWithinPriority verifies missing queue-rank sorts after explicit ranks in the same priority bucket.
5. TestQueueRankNumericStringCompatibility verifies numeric-string queue-rank values are read compatibly.
6. WIRED-IN: sortBeadsForQueue, used by Ready and ReadyExecution, calls the queue-rank helper before created_at/id tie-breakers.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run "TestReady.*QueueRank|TestQueueRank" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:beads, kind:feature, spec:FEAT-004</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-0a47840f" spec-id="FEAT-004" base-rev="74060212ac5681895023c2df2d37ae7c4dd6fb7f" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T133547-af606d56"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-004" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md">Feature: Beads (Work Item Tracker)</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `cccc05f` (`fix: order ready beads by queue-rank [ddx-5ca2b0af]`).

What changed:
- Added queue-rank parsing and queue ordering in [cli/internal/bead/store.go](absolute:/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-5ca2b0af-20260506T133547-af606d56/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L1702), so ready sorting is now:
  - priority
  - explicit `queue-rank`
  - created_at
  - id
- Added focused coverage in [cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go](absolute:/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-5ca2b0af-20260506T133547-af606d56/cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go#L58) for:
  - ready ordering within a priority bucket
  - ReadyExecution ordering
  - priority boundary protection
  - missing-rank placement after ranked beads
  - numeric-string compatibility

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestReady.*QueueRank|TestQueueRank' -count=1`
- `go test ./internal/bead/... -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`
